title.en: Scarlet Leopard with One Horn and Five Tails

summary.zh: 章莪之山的怪兽，形如赤豹，独角五尾，吼声像两块石头相击。
summary.en: A beast of Mount Zhang'e shaped like a scarlet leopard, with a single horn and five tails, its voice like stones striking together.

description.zh:
《西次三经》记章莪之山无草木，多瑶碧，“所为甚怪”——是一座盛产美玉而怪事频出的石山。山中有兽，“其状如赤豹，五尾一角，其音如击石，其名曰狰”：通体赤红如豹，头顶一只独角，身后五条尾巴，吼声像两块石头相击。

《山海经》描述异兽，惯以肢体的增减为记：狰的一角五尾，与同经中一目三尾的讙、三首六尾的鵸鵌属于同一套造形语言。狰与预示怪火的独脚鸟毕方同住章莪之山；经文没有给它安排任何吉凶兆验，也没有食用佩服之效，在动辄“见则大旱”“服之已瘅”的西山经诸兽中，这份沉默反而少见。明清各种《山海经》图本多把狰画作立于山石之上的五尾独角豹，这一形象也进入了现代的图鉴、游戏与插画。
description.en:
Mount Zhang'e, says the 'Classic of the Western Mountains', bears no plants but abounds in fine jade, and 'strange things happen there'. Among them prowls the Zheng: 'shaped like a scarlet leopard, with five tails and a single horn, its voice like the striking of stones.'

The Shanhaijing habitually marks its beasts by adding or subtracting body parts, and the Zheng — one horn, five tails — belongs to the same figural grammar as the one-eyed, three-tailed Huan and the three-headed, six-tailed Qitu elsewhere in the same classic. It shares Mount Zhang'e with the Bifang, the one-legged bird of fire omens, yet the text assigns the Zheng itself no omen, no cure, and no use — an unusual silence in a book where most western beasts either portend drought and flood or heal ills when eaten or worn. Ming and Qing illustrated editions draw it as a five-tailed, horned leopard poised on bare rock, an image that has passed into modern bestiaries, games, and illustration.

value.en: A scarlet leopard with five tails and one horn

value.zh: 章莪之山，无草木多瑶碧
value.en: Mount Zhang'e, barren but rich in jade
